title: WII Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 38 Project Assistant, Project Scientist and More Posts
shortInfo: Wildlife Institute of India (WII) Project Positions Notification 2026 has been released on 15-05-2026 on the official website. A total of 38 vacancies have been announced through the WII Project Positions Recruitment 2026. Candidates aged between 18 to 35 years who have a Doctoral Degree in Natural Sciences (Wildlife Science/Zoology/Ecology/Environmental Sciences/Geoinformatics/Life Sciences)/Forestry Sciences, Master's Degree in relevant fields, or Bachelor's Degree in relevant fields are eligible to apply for these Project Positions vacancies. The online application process for WII Project P

basic_details.job_title: WII Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 38 Project Assistant, Project Scientist and More Posts
basic_details.post_name: Project Assistant, Project Scientist and More
basic_details.short_information: Wildlife Institute of India (WII) Project Positions Notification 2026 has been released on 15-05-2026 on the official website. A total of 38 vacancies have been announced through the WII Project Positions Recruitment 2026. Candidates aged between 18 to 35 years who have a Doctoral Degree in Natural Sciences (Wildlife Science/Zoology/Ecology/Environmental Sciences/Geoinformatics/Life Sciences)/Forestry Sciences, Master's Degree in relevant fields, or Bachelor's Degree in relevant fields are eligible to apply for these Project Positions vacancies. The online application process for WII Project P

qualification.education_qualification: Doctoral Degree in Natural Sciences (Wildlife Science/Zoology/Ecology/Environmental Sciences/Geoinformatics/Life Sciences)/Forestry Sciences, Master's Degree in relevant fields, Bachelor's Degree in relevant fields
qualification.details: Doctoral Degree in Natural Sciences (Wildlife Science / Zoology / Ecology / Environmental Sciences / Geoinformatics / Life Sciences) / Forestry Sciences / Social Sciences from a recognized institute / university (varies by position). Master's Degree in relevant fields from a recognized institute / university (varies by position). Bachelor's Degree in relevant fields from a recognized institute / university (varies by position).

salary_details.pay_scale: Rs. 24,000 + HRA - Rs. 77,000 + HRA Per Month
salary_details.details: The salary for the Project Positions ranges from Rs. 24,000 + HRA to Rs. 77,000 + HRA Per Month, depending on the specific position and qualifications.

selection_process: Online application submission and fee payment. Screening and shortlisting based on academics
publications etc. Online interview. Document verification.
how_to_apply: Candidates who meet the eligibility criteria can apply online for the WII Project Positions Recruitment 2026.
Applications must be submitted online on or before 29-05-2026.
To apply directly, use the Apply Online link provided in the Important Links section.
Alternatively, candidates may visit the official website of WII to complete the application.
Fill out the application form carefully with correct personal, educational, and contact details.
Upload required documents such as photograph, signature, and certificates in the prescribed format.
Pay the application fee online, if applicable.
Review all details carefully before submitting the form.
Take a printout or save a copy of the submitted application for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for WII Project Positions Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit your online application for the WII Project Positions is 29 May 2026. Ensure you complete the process before this deadline to be considered.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available for Project Positions at WII?
faq.answer: Wildlife Institute of India (WII) has announced a total of 38 vacancies for Project Positions in their latest recruitment drive for 2026.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for these positions?
faq.answer: The eligibility criteria include Doctoral Degree, Master's Degree, or Bachelor's Degree in relevant fields such as Wildlife Science, Zoology, Ecology, Environmental Sciences, Geoinformatics, Life Sciences, Forestry Sciences, or Social Sciences.

faq.question: Q4. What is the application fee for WII Project Positions?
faq.answer: The application fee is Rs. 500/- for General/OBC category candidates and Rs. 100/- for SC/ST category candidates.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary range for the selected candidates?
faq.answer: Selected candidates will receive a monthly salary ranging from Rs. 24,000 + HRA to Rs. 77,000 + HRA, depending on the specific post and their qualifications.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for WII Project Positions?
faq.answer: The selection process involves online application submission, screening and shortlisting based on academic records and experience, followed by an online interview and document verification.

faq.question: Q7. Is there any age relaxation for reserved categories?
faq.answer: Yes, SC/ST candidates are eligible for a 5-year age relaxation, and OBC candidates are eligible for a 3-year age relaxation, as per Government of India rules.

faq.question: Q8. Can I apply offline for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No, applications for the WII Project Positions Recruitment 2026 must be submitted strictly through the online mode. Offline applications will not be accepted.

faq.question: Q9. Where can I find the official notification for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The official notification PDF can be downloaded from the Important Links section of this article. It contains all the detailed information regarding the recruitment.

faq.question: Q10. What is the job type for these positions?
faq.answer: The positions offered are contractual in nature, providing an opportunity to work on specific projects within the Wildlife Institute of India.
